moveAnnotations.annotation: We're playing the Van't Kruijs Opening, one of the most unusual first moves in chess! By advancing the e-pawn just one square instead of the standard two, we're playing a quiet, flexible system that avoids the theory-heavy main lines. This move controls the d4 and f4 squares (which will be important for our pieces), keeps our options open, and invites our opponent to choose the battleground. While it's not as forcing as 1.e4 or 1.d4, the Van't Kruijs has been played by strong players as a practical weapon against unprepared opponents—you're essentially saying 'let's play chess, not memorized opening theory.' The setup typically leads to positions where we'll fianchetto our bishop and develop flexibly, so we need patience and good positional understanding rather than tactical brilliance.
